Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Show Cause Re: Non-Compliance With Investigative Subpoenas (Gov. Code 11188)
Off Calendar Apr-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
DIANE J. KIM, Esq. Deputy Attorney General (415) 510-4429 diane.kim@doj.ca.gov; Attorney for Petitioner The People of the State of California
CHRISTOPHER T. JOHNSON, Esq., GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP (619) 696-6700 bledger@grsm.com Attorneys for Respondents GREENPOWER MOTOR COMPANY, INC. and SAN JOAQUIN VALLEY EQUIPMENT LEASING, INC.for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA EX REL. ROB's Motion For An Order To Show Cause Re: Non-Compliance With Investigative Subpoenas (Gov. Code 11188)

Petitioner's motion for an order to show cause regarding alleged non-compliance with state administrative subpoenas is granted subject to an appropriate briefing schedule.

Respondents contend, without citation of authority or identification of prejudice, that the motion should be denied because it was filed before Respondents had appeared in the action. That contention is without merit in the context of administrative subpoenas and an enforcement action.

Respondents further contend, again without citation of authority, that the motion should be denied because there is a demurrer pending which may result in a finding that this Court lacks jurisdiction. That contention is likewise without merit. Civil discovery can typically be commenced before responsive pleadings are due (Code of Civil Procedure Sections 2025.210(b), 2030.020(b), and 2031.020(b)) and responses to administrative subpoenas may be due without any pending action at all. Parties commonly conduct discovery to develop their pleadings and do so even after a demurrer has been sustained with leave to amend. Budget Finance Plan v. Superior Court, 34 Cal. App. 3d 7994, 797-798 (1973); Union Mutual Life v. Superior Court, 80 Cal.App.3d 1, 12 (1978). At this point, it is unknown whether the demurrer will be sustained and if so whether with or without leave to amend. The following briefing schedule applies: Hearing re: Order to Show Cause is set for May 22, 2024 at 9:00 a.m. Dept. 302 Discovery; Respondent's opening brief due April 30, 2024; Petitioners opposition due by May 9, 2024; Respondent's reply due May 15, 2024.

Prevailing party shall submit a proposed order in Word format to the Temporary Judge at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
